core_mechanism: Microscopic weak-coupling (Born-Markov) reduction of a subsystem-plus-reservoir dynamics to a closed linear generator (Markovian master equation) for the subsystem by tracing out the environment, deriving the dissipative terms from the underlying coupling while retaining the fast, non-secular cross-terms oscillating at the system's characteristic frequencies instead of averaging them away.
brief_reason: The abstract gives a microscopic derivation of the master equation for an open system with losses that keeps the dissipator terms varying at frequencies of order the vacuum Rabi frequency, i.e. it derives the dissipative generator from first principles while going beyond the usual secular/rotating-wave truncation.
